Posts:
🚆 How do you make AI part of daily operations, not just a slide in a strategy deck?

At NS, director of data, innovation and AI, Daan Debie, shared how AI now supports real decisions on trains and stations. Think crowdedness predictions and bike-availability estimates that help passengers move smoothly through the network.

His key message: real impact comes when AI, people, and processes grow together.

🔑 NS focuses on:
• Strong governance that sets clear responsibilities and guardrails.
• Comprehensive training so staff understand, trust, and improve AI systems.
• User involvement from day one, so solutions fit operational reality.

That approach turns pilots into live systems that support safety, reliability, and a better passenger experience.

At KickstartAI, we see NS as a powerful example of how Dutch organizations can scale AI responsibly, in line with European values and the EU AI Act.

European leadership in AI will come from this kind of practical, grounded progress in operations.

👉 Which operational decision in your organization would benefit most from AI support?

Posts:
Coding agents can write code fast. The real challenge is keeping quality high and staying in control of what lands in your repo. 🧪📂

Our latest KickstartAI article explores how to work with coding agents in a way that fits professional engineering standards.

Key ideas:

🔹 Stay in the driver’s seat
Treat the agent as a powerful assistant, not an autonomous developer. Review, refactor, and make architectural decisions yourself.

🔹 Let tests guide the agent
Well-designed tests give the agent a clear target and give your team a safety net for future changes.

🔹 Watch out for “silent collapse”
Agents can confidently produce code that compiles but behaves incorrectly. Regular checks and small, testable steps reduce that risk.

🔹 Plan for long-term maintainability
Structure prompts, tools, and workflows so that another engineer can understand and extend the AI-generated code.

By combining coding agents with disciplined engineering practices, Dutch organizations can scale AI responsibly and build systems that last.

Curious how to put this into practice in your team? 👇
https://kickstart.ai/news/agentic-engineering-best-practices-coding-agents-avoid-slop-increase-productivity

#ResponsibleAI #AICoding #NLTech #KickstartAI

Coding agents change how code is written, but the most important change happens in how developers work. 👩‍💻👨‍💻

In our latest piece, we look at what happens when agents take over more of the typing, and developers step into a new role:

🔹 Developers spend more time reviewing, testing, and shaping requirements.
🔹 Communication skills become as important as language syntax knowledge.
🔹 Teams invest in better documentation, so agents and humans share context.
🔹 Product leaders focus on designing workflows where agents add value, not noise.

For the Netherlands, this evolution supports a more resilient AI workforce, where people and agents complement each other. Developers move closer to product decisions, and organizations gain capacity to experiment responsibly with AI.

At KickstartAI, we collaborate with companies to prepare teams for this shift and to share lessons across the community.

Ready to see how your development role might look in an agent-powered team? 🔍

Posts:
KLM is moving AI out of the lab and into daily operations. ✈️

In a recent talk, CIO Aart Slagt shared how the airline now focuses on deploying AI where it truly matters: planning, optimization, and better service for passengers.

KLM already runs AI models that help predict catering needs and optimize contingency fuel. The impact is clear: smarter operations, less waste, and more reliable service.

Aart emphasized one key principle: an AI model only creates value when people understand it and trust it. That means clear explanations, strong involvement of end users, and space for feedback.

KLM also built “Talia,” a secure environment where employees can work with AI tools safely and confidently. That kind of trusted sandbox helps large organizations explore, learn, and scale responsibly.

At KickstartAI, we see this as a blueprint for the Dutch ecosystem: focus on concrete use cases, invest in user trust, and build secure AI environments that enable innovation.

Responsible deployment at this scale does not happen in isolation. It grows from collaboration between corporate teams, domain experts, and the wider AI community.

🚀 Want to help shape the next wave of applied AI in the Netherlands?
Join the conversation, share your AI deployment stories, and let’s learn together.

GenAI in financial services is moving fast, and many teams feel the pressure to experiment while staying within regulatory boundaries. At KickstartAI, we see that responsible innovation is very achievable with the right collaboration model. 🤝

In our joint work with ING on a financial health assistant, three elements proved crucial:

💡 Shared responsibility
Product owners, legal, risk, and engineers co-own design choices around explainability, data use, and tone of voice.

📏 Built-in guardrails
Technical safeguards, evaluation pipelines, and clear escalation paths help keep responses accurate, scoped, and fair.

📚 Learning mindset
Teams treat GenAI as an evolving capability, updating prompts, training data, and policies as new patterns and regulations emerge.

By combining these elements, organizations can create AI services that improve financial health, strengthen trust, and fit the EU AI Act context.

At KickstartAI, we aim to turn such collaborations into reusable patterns for the Dutch ecosystem.

🔗 Read the full story and lessons here:
https://kickstart.ai/news/why-managing-genai-projects-requires-playbook-tweak

Many AI pilots look promising on day one. The real challenge starts on Day Two.

Leon Dusée from ING calls out a familiar failure point: teams build a great proof of concept, but no one plans how to run, monitor, and maintain it at scale.

At KickstartAI, we see this Day Two problem across sectors in the Dutch ecosystem.

Successful AI products answer questions like:

⚙️ "Who owns this model once it goes live?"
📊 "How will we monitor performance and data drift over time?"
🔐 "How do we stay compliant with the EU AI Act as we iterate?"
🤝 "How do business, data, and IT teams collaborate around this solution?"

Organizations that design for Day Two from the start move faster, reduce risk, and unlock AI impact that lasts.

KickstartAI partners with companies to turn pilots into production-ready solutions that are maintainable, compliant, and aligned with real-world processes. Shared expertise across industries helps everyone avoid reinventing the wheel.

AI adoption in the Netherlands accelerates when fewer pilots stall and more solutions reach everyday use.

Ready to tackle your Day Two challenges together? 🚀

Environmental crime affects nature, neighborhoods, and trust in institutions. Investigators face huge volumes of legislation, case law, and documents for every case. 

Together with the Dutch National Police, KickstartAI is co-developing an AI assistant that helps investigators quickly navigate relevant laws, historic cases, and patterns in earlier investigations. The goal is simple: give experts better information, faster.

👮‍♂️ Police investigators stay in control.
🤖 AI surfaces connections and similar cases in seconds.
⚖️ Human judgment, transparency, and explainability guide every decision.

Senior innovation advisor Amir Niknam shared how his team approaches AI in this high-stakes context: use AI as a practical tool, keep humans in the lead, and design for legal robustness from day one.

At KickstartAI, we believe that responsible AI can strengthen the rule of law and help protect the environment. By bringing public-sector experts and AI practitioners together, the Netherlands can turn complex challenges into concrete, working solutions.

Posts:
Reliable AI in practice starts with rigorous work in research labs and universities.

🧪 The DataDivers project, led by Associate Professor Dong Nguyen, focuses on one key question: how can we measure diversity in language model datasets in a systematic way? The project looks at stylistic and semantic diversity and studies how these factors affect model performance and behavior.

That kind of clarity is very useful for organizations that want to deploy AI responsibly. When teams understand which types of language, topics, and styles a dataset covers, they can better judge where a model will perform well, and where it might miss important perspectives.

For Dutch organizations working under new European rules, this creates a practical bridge between regulation, ethics, and engineering.

KickstartAI supports this type of research because it turns abstract principles into tools that data scientists and product teams can actually use.

If you work with language data and want AI that serves a diverse society, you will want to follow this work closely 👇

#AI #NLP #Fairness #DataQuality #Netherlands

🌍 Space data is turning into public infrastructure, and AI is making it usable for everyone.

Open satellite programs and affordable commercial imagery now provide continuous coverage of our cities, coasts, and countryside. AI takes those pixels and turns them into indicators that policymakers and entrepreneurs can use every day.

In the Dutch context, that means local governments and startups can:

• Explore scenarios for housing development without waiting for new field surveys.
• Track water quality and flood risk more frequently and consistently.
• Build new services that quantify environmental impact for citizens and clients.

Public access to satellite data gives many players the same shared starting point. AI models built on top create a common language for evaluating options and measuring results.

KickstartAI supports this evolution by bringing together experts, public partners, and innovators to test AI solutions on real Dutch challenges. Collaborative experimentation shortens the path from raw data to tools that professionals can trust in daily decisions.

🚀 When space data and AI become part of the standard toolkit, the whole ecosystem benefits.

#AI #EarthObservation #GovTech #OpenData #Innovation

Posts:
How do you turn a complex aviation question into a concrete AI solution? You bring the ecosystem together. ✈️🤝

In project Daidalos, KickstartAI teamed up with KLM and 12 data scientists from several Dutch organizations to explore one clear challenge:

👉 How can machine learning improve contingency fuel planning for each flight?

Over an intensive period, the team:
• Tested multiple models, with LightGBM performing especially well after smart data refinement.
• Discovered that detailed weather data contributed less than expected to prediction quality.
• Built explainability tools so pilots and dispatchers can understand, question, and trust the model.

The result is a promising approach that supports safer, more efficient fuel decisions, while opening the door to lower emissions and costs.

Next up, KLM will validate the solution with business stakeholders and explore how it can fit daily operations.

At KickstartAI, we believe the strongest AI solutions in the Netherlands come from shared challenges, shared data, and shared learning.

Want to join future challenges or explore a collaboration? Our inbox is open. 👇

#AI #Aviation #Sustainability #DataScience #Netherlands

• Building AI solutions together with our partners that solve real problems.

🌱 Multimodal AI for biodiversity is moving from concept to concrete impact.

In our latest article, we explore two complementary initiatives: BioAnalyst at TNO and the TATTOOs project at LIACS. Together, they show how combining data sources and new vision techniques leads to richer biodiversity insights.

BioAnalyst brings climate, species, and environmental data into one forecasting model. TATTOOs uses bio-inspired computer vision to recognize individual animals without physical tracking.

For policymakers, researchers, and practitioners, these tools open doors to:

📊 More accurate biodiversity indicators to guide conservation strategy.
📡 More scalable monitoring approaches that work across regions.
🤝 Stronger collaboration across institutes, supported by shared models and open research.

KickstartAI champions projects like these because they match our mission: accelerate AI adoption in the Netherlands, and help organizations apply AI responsibly for societal good.

We see huge potential when Dutch research institutes, public organizations, and companies join forces around open, practical AI solutions.

🌱 How can multimodal AI deepen our understanding of biodiversity?
 
 In our latest article, we explore two initiatives at Dutch research institutes: BioAnalyst at TNO and the TATTOOs project at LIACS.
 
 We speak with AI Research Scientist Thanasis Trantas at TNO and Assistant Professor Rita Pucci at LIACS about how these models work, the challenges behind them, and what they mean for biodiversity research.
 
 BioAnalyst unites climate, species, and environmental data into one forecasting model. TATTOOs uses bio-inspired computer vision to recognize individual animals by the unique patterns on their skin.
 
 Together, these projects show what becomes possible when researchers combine data sources with new vision techniques:
 
 📊 More accurate biodiversity indicators that guide conservation strategy.
 📡 Scalable monitoring that works across regions.
 🤝 Shared models and open research that strengthen collaboration between institutes.
 
 We see real momentum when Dutch research institutes, public organizations, and companies work side by side on practical, responsible AI. That spirit sits at the heart of our mission to accelerate AI adoption in the Netherlands and apply AI for societal good.
